description: Concurrent use significantly increases the risk of bleeding due to additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects.
recommendation: Avoid combination unless specifically indicated. Monitor INR closely if co-administration is necessary.

description: NSAIDs increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin and risk of GI bleeding.
recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If necessary, monitor INR frequently and watch for signs of bleeding.

description: Amoxicillin may increase the anticoagulant effect of warfarin by altering gut flora and vitamin K production.
recommendation: Monitor INR more frequently when starting or stopping amoxicillin.

description: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin, significantly increasing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 25-50%. Monitor INR closely.

description: Omeprazole may inhibit CYP2C19 metabolism and slightly increase warfarin levels.
recommendation: Monitor INR when starting or stopping omeprazole therapy.

description: Ibuprofen may interfere with the antiplatelet effect of low-dose aspirin. Both increase risk of GI bleeding.
recommendation: Take aspirin at least 30 minutes before ibuprofen. Consider alternative analgesic.

description: Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk, though combination is often intentional post-ACS.
recommendation: Combination is standard after acute coronary syndrome but increases bleeding risk. Monitor for signs of bleeding.

description: ACE inhibitor + potassium-sparing diuretic significantly increases risk of hyperkalemia.
recommendation: Monitor serum potassium closely. Avoid in patients with renal impairment.

description: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone, decreasing potassium excretion. Additional potassium supplementation may cause dangerous hyperkalemia.
recommendation: Monitor potassium levels frequently. Avoid potassium supplements unless documented hypokalemia.

description: NSAIDs may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase risk of renal impairment.
recommendation: Monitor blood pressure and renal function. Consider alternative analgesic.

description: Iodinated contrast media may cause acute kidney injury, leading to metformin accumulation and lactic acidosis.
recommendation: Discontinue metformin 48 hours before and after contrast administration. Check renal function before restarting.

description: Alcohol potentiates the effect of metformin on lactate metabolism, increasing risk of lactic acidosis.
recommendation: Limit alcohol consumption. Avoid binge drinking. Educate patient on symptoms of lactic acidosis.

description: Clarithromycin is a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor that dramatically increases statin levels, risking rhabdomyolysis.
recommendation: Contraindicated. Use azithromycin as alternative antibiotic, or temporarily hold statin.

description: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4 and increases simvastatin levels, increasing risk of myopathy.
recommendation: Limit simvastatin dose to 20 mg/day when used with amlodipine.

description: Grapefruit juice inhibits CYP3A4 in the gut wall, increasing statin absorption and levels.
recommendation: Avoid large amounts of grapefruit juice. Small amounts are generally acceptable.

description: Aluminum/magnesium antacids chelate ciprofloxacin, dramatically reducing its absorption and efficacy.
recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin at least 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids.

description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 metabolism of theophylline, causing toxic theophylline levels.
recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by 30-50%. Monitor theophylline levels closely.

description: Both drugs increase serotonin levels. Combination increases risk of serotonin syndrome.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If necessary, monitor for agitation, hyperthermia, tachycardia, and clonus.

description: Combination causes severe serotonin syndrome, potentially fatal. Both drugs increase serotonin dramatically.
recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Wait at least 5 weeks after stopping fluoxetine before starting MAOI (due to long half-life of norfluoxetine).

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome due to additive serotonergic effects.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use alternative analgesic without serotonergic activity.

description: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70-100% by reducing renal and non-renal clearance.
recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels.

description: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin, significantly increasing INR.
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50%. Monitor INR weekly until stable.

description: Additive negative chronotropic and inotropic effects may cause severe bradycardia, heart block, or heart failure.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If essential, monitor ECG and hemodynamics closely.

description: Furosemide-induced hypokalemia increases sensitivity to digoxin toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor potassium levels. Supplement potassium as needed. Watch for signs of digoxin toxicity.

description: Combined CNS depression increases risk of profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death.
recommendation: FDA Black Box Warning: avoid concurrent use unless no alternative. Use lowest effective doses and shortest duration.

description: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of lithium, increasing serum levels by 15-50%.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs if possible. If necessary, monitor lithium levels closely and reduce dose.

description: ACE inhibitors reduce renal clearance of lithium, causing toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor lithium levels frequently. Consider dose reduction.

description: Both are folate antagonists. Combination significantly increases risk of bone marrow suppression.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If essential, monitor CBC closely and supplement with folinic acid.

description: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risk.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs during methotrexate therapy. Use acetaminophen for pain if needed.

description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing conversion of clopidogrel to its active metabolite.
recommendation: Use pantoprazole instead of omeprazole. Pantoprazole has minimal CYP2C19 interaction.

description: Severe additive hypotension. Both cause vasodilation via the nitric oxide pathway.
recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Do not administer nitrates within 24 hours of sildenafil (48 hours for tadalafil).

description: Spironolactone is potassium-sparing. Additional potassium supplementation risks severe hyperkalemia.
recommendation: Avoid combination unless documented hypokalemia. Monitor potassium levels closely.

description: Erythromycin is a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor that significantly increases statin levels, risking rhabdomyolysis.
recommendation: Temporarily discontinue simvastatin during erythromycin course. Use azithromycin as alternative.

description: Amoxicillin may reduce renal tubular secretion of methotrexate, increasing its levels and toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor methotrexate levels and CBC. Consider dose adjustment.

description: Chronic alcohol use induces CYP2E1, increasing formation of hepatotoxic NAPQI metabolite of paracetamol.
recommendation: Limit paracetamol to 2 g/day in patients with regular alcohol consumption. Avoid in heavy drinkers.

description: Beta-blockers may mask symptoms of hypoglycemia (tachycardia, tremor) and prolong hypoglycemic episodes.
recommendation: Use cardioselective beta-blockers (metoprolol, bisoprolol). Educate patient to monitor blood glucose more frequently.

description: Amlodipine may increase cyclosporine levels. Cyclosporine may increase amlodipine levels.
recommendation: Monitor cyclosporine trough levels. Adjust doses as needed.

description: Combined use increases risk of GI ulceration and bleeding.
recommendation: Add gastroprotection (PPI) if combination is necessary. Monitor for GI symptoms.

description: Both drugs prolong QT interval. Combination increases risk of torsades de pointes.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Monitor ECG if co-administration is unavoidable.

description: Divalent cations in antacids chelate doxycycline, reducing absorption by up to 50%.
recommendation: Separate administration by at least 2-3 hours.

description: Complex interaction: valproate displaces phenytoin from protein binding and inhibits its metabolism.
recommendation: Monitor free phenytoin levels (not total). Adjust doses based on clinical response.

description: Erythromycin inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism of carbamazepine, causing toxic levels.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use azithromycin as alternative antibiotic.

description: Metoclopramide is a dopamine antagonist that directly counteracts the therapeutic effect of levodopa.
recommendation: Contraindicated in Parkinson's disease. Use domperidone as antiemetic alternative.
